title: New novels from Elizabeth Strout and 'Pemi Aguda are lonely and enchanting
summary: Aguda's novel, One Leg on Earth, follows a young woman in Nigeria facing an unintended pregnancy. The Things We Never Say, by Strout, centers on a high school teacher leading a secret life of sadness.

title: She spent a year using AI to do almost everything. Here's what she learned
summary: Tech writer Joanna Stern used AI to read medical results, respond to texts and serve as her therapist. She says her emotional connection to it was unsettling. Her new book is I Am Not a Robot.

title: In 'The Young Will Remember,' a Korean War reporter gets stranded behind enemy lines
summary: In Eve J. Chung's new novel, Ellie Chang ends up stuck in a place she's only known as enemy territory, reliant on strangers to help her get home.

title: Novelists Tayari Jones and Meg Wolitzer share notes on mothers in fiction
summary: Ahead of Mother's Day, we brought together two novelists -- and friends -- Tayari Jones and Meg Wolitzer, to talk about what they love about reading and writing about mothers.

title: New book reimagines Hans Christian Andersen's visit with Charles Dickens and family
summary: In Five Weeks in the Country, author Francine Prose imagines a distressing, ill-timed visit to Charles Dickens' home in 1857 — offering a memorable twist on the classic English country house drama.

title: Scott Turow's latest real-life legal thriller: Suing Meta for copyright infringement
summary: Five major publishing houses and the bestselling author are suing Meta and its CEO Mark Zuckerberg for allegedly training its Llama generative AI models on millions of copyrighted materials.

title: A legal scholar and 'Backtalker' defends critical race theory — a term she helped coin
summary: Kimberlé Williams Crenshaw helped name two contested ideas in U.S. politics — intersectionality and critical race theory. Her memoir chronicles the personal and legal framework for her thinking.

title: It's blockbuster book season: Here are 12 new titles coming in May
summary: Summer blockbuster season has begun — on the big screen and in bookstores. This month brings new titles from Douglas Stuart, Kathryn Stockett, Ali Smith, David Sedaris and many more.

title: Here are the 2026 Pulitzer Prize winners
summary: Award winners include authors Daniel Kraus, Jill Lepore and Yiyun Li, opinion writer M. Gessen and staffers and contributors at The Washington Post, Reuters and AP.

title: In 'John of John,' father and son are gay -- and they're keeping it from each other
summary: Like a number of his characters, Booker Prize-winning novelist Douglas Stuart grew up working class and queer in Glasgow. He went on to have a career in fashion, which plays into his latest novel.
